column1: # Smartwatch Firmware — Partition Table
# Target: ESP32-S3
# OTA STRATEGY: Full-image swap only (factory → ota_0 → ota_1 → ota_0 …)
#   - factory: shipped firmware / factory reset target
#   - ota_0 / ota_1: A/B swap slots for OTA updates
#   - otadata: tracks which slot to boot from (managed by ESP-IDF OTA library)
# FLASH USAGE SUMMARY (16 MB total):
#   Bootloader (default):   ~32 KB  (0x0000 – 0x7FFF
#   Partition table:         4 KB   (0x8000 – 0x8FFF
#   nvs:                    16 KB   ← sensor cal
#   otadata:                 8 KB   ← OTA boot slot tracking
#   phy_init:                4 KB   ← RF calibration (required even if WiFi unused now)
#   factory:                 2 MB   ← baseline firmware
#   ota_0:                   2 MB   ← first OTA target slot
#   ota_1:                   2 MB   ← second OTA target slot (swap back capability)
#   spiffs / remaining:    ~9.9 MB  ← available for future assets
# SIZING NOTES:
#   2 MB app partitions are generous for this firmware. The current build
#   is well under 1 MB. Headroom exists for BSEC
#   If the build grows beyond 2 MB
column2: increase all three app partitions equally

column1: #   and adjust offsets — the 16 MB flash has ample room.
# WIFI / OTA READINESS:
#   This table is OTA-ready from day one. When the WiFi+OTA module is added:
#     1. Enable esp_https_ota component in CMakeLists.txt
#     2. Call esp_ota_begin() / esp_ota_write() / esp_ota_end() from the OTA task
#     3. No partition table changes required
#     4. For factory reset: call nvs_flash_erase() then esp_ota_set_boot_partition(factory)
# FORMAT: Name
# Offsets left blank = auto-assigned sequentially by esptool (recommended)
# Name
nvs
phy_init
factory
storage
